paper.title: A study of the relaxation dynamics in a quadrupolar NMR system using Quantum State Tomography

implemented_equations.synthetic_channel: rho(t) = dephase_q[ exp(-gamma_pop t) rho0 + (1-exp(-gamma_pop t)) I/d ]
implemented_equations.coherence_decay: |rho_ij(t)| ~ exp[-(gamma_pop + gamma_phi q^2)t], q = m_i - m_j
implemented_equations.tomography: A vec(rho) = b solved by least squares with trace constraint

assumptions: Two synthetic initial states are used: population-biased and coherent-superposition.
Initial states are slightly mixed to avoid singular fidelity artifacts.
The reproduction uses synthetic QST data because no time-resolved experimental QST dataset is present yet.
The paper result is reproduced as a workflow: QST time series -> density-matrix elements -> fitted relaxation rates.
